sections.paragraph:
(“one who forgets;” see Gen 41:51), pr.n. Gr. Μανασσῆς, Manasseh.-

(1) a son of Joseph, adopted by Jacob his grandfather, Gen 48:1, seqq. For the boundaries of the territory of the tribe of Manasseh, part of which was on each side of Jordan, see -Josh. 13:2932 ; 17:8 , seqq. Patron. מְנַשִּׁי Deu 4:43.-(2) a king of Judah (699-644, B.C.), the son of Hezekiah, remarkable for his idolatry, superstition, and cruelty towards the righteous, -2 Kings 21:118 ; -2 Ch. 33:120 .-(3) Jud. 18:30 כתיב.-(4) Ezr 10:30.-(5) verse 33 Ezr 10:33.
